[{'role': 'system', 'content': 'You are an expert in the AVISPA tool. I will give you a hlpsl language file of a protocol. Please help me add the notation to it. The requirement is that the original code should remain unchanged and the added notations are semantically correct. Also, the final hlpsl code file can be run by AVISPA, and located between ```hlpsl and ```.'}, {'role': 'user', 'content': 'The content of the hlpsl file is as follows. 
```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G12 (Confidentiality) G2 (Authentication)
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,  % Shared password-derived key
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,         % Protocol state machine
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce and challenge generated by initiator
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge from responder
         X,K  : message      % Received message and computed session key

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Secrecy identifiers

  init   State := 0

  transition
    %%% PHASE 1: Key Exchange Initiation %%%
    % Start signal received -> Send g^Na mod p
    1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Generate fresh nonce
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
    %%% PHASE 2: Responder's Key Component Processing %%%
    % Receive g^Nb mod p -> Compute session key K = (g^Nb)^Na
    2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na) % Compute shared secret
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send encrypted challenge
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Register Ca secrecy
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Authentication witness for Ca

    %%% PHASE 3: Responder Authentication %%%
    % Receive encrypted {Cb,Ca} -> Verify and respond
    3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)     % Return encrypted Cb
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Register Cb secrecy
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Verify responder's Cb

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,  % Shared password-derived key
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,          % Protocol state machine
        Nb,Cb: text,         % Nonce and challenge generated by responder
        Ca   : text,         % Challenge from initiator
        Y,K  : message       % Received message and computed session key

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Secrecy identifiers

  init  State := 0

  transition
    %%% PHASE 1: Key Exchange Response %%%
    % Receive g^Na mod p -> Send g^Nb mod p
    1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()     % Generate fresh nonce
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) 
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb') % Compute shared secret

    %%% PHASE 2: Initiator Challenge Processing %%%
    % Receive {Ca}_K -> Verify and respond with {Cb,Ca}
    2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()     % Generate challenge
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K) % Return both challenges
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Register Ca secrecy
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Register Cb secrecy
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Authentication witness for Cb
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Verify initiator's Ca

    %%% PHASE 3: Initiator Authentication Verification %%%
    % Receive {Cb}_K -> Final authentication
    3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3  % Protocol completion

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition
       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initiator instance
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Responder instance

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,        % Honest participants
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key, % Shared keys (kab=legitimate)
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Initial intruder knowledge

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)    % Legitimate session
    /\  session(a,i,kai) % Initiator with intruder
    /\  session(i,b,kbi) % Responder with intruder

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal
   % G12: Confidentiality properties
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Initiator's secrets
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb   % Responder's secrets

   % G2: Authentication properties
   authentication_on cb  % Responder authenticates to Initiator
   authentication_on ca  % Initiator authenticates to Responder

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
```

Key additions:
1. **Role Parameters**: Added comments for shared keys (`Kab`) and channels
2. **State Variables**: Clarified purpose of nonces (`Na`, `Nb`) and challenges (`Ca`, `Cb`)
3. **Transition Phases**: Marked 3 protocol phases with `%%%` comments
4. **Security Properties**: Annotated G12 (confidentiality) and G2 (authentication) goals
5. **Environment Setup**: Explained intruder knowledge and session compositions
6. **Computation Notes**: Added comments for key derivation (`K' := exp(X',Na)`)
7. **Security Annotations**: Clarified `witness`/`request` purpose for authentication

The added notations:
- Preserve original HLPSL syntax
- Explain security mechanisms without altering behavior
- Maintain AVISPA compatibility
- Follow SPEKE protocol semantics from the reference paper
- Highlight confidentiality (G12) and authentication (G2) goals

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,             % Current state of the initiator
         Na,Ca: text,            % Nonce and challenge generated by A
         Cb   : text,            % Challenge received from B
         X,K  : message          % Temporary variables for received message and computed key

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol identifiers for secrecy properties

  init   State := 0              % Initial state

  transition

      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new() % Generate fresh nonce Na
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) % Send exponential of shared key with nonce
        
   % Transition 2: Receive response from responder and send challenge
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new() % Generate fresh challenge Ca
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na) % Compute session key K
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send encrypted challenge
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Mark Ca as secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca') % Authentication property for Ca

   % Transition 3: Receive response to challenge and complete protocol
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K) % Send back encrypted Cb to confirm
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Mark Cb as secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb') % Authentication property for Cb

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,              % Current state of the responder
        Nb,Cb: text,             % Nonce and challenge generated by B
        Ca   : text,             % Challenge received from A
        Y,K  : message           % Temporary variables for received message and computed key

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id % Protocol identifiers for secrecy properties

  init  State := 0               % Initial state

  transition

   % Transition 1: Receive initial message and respond
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new() % Generate fresh nonce Nb
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send exponential of shared key with nonce
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb') % Compute session key K

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new() % Generate fresh challenge Cb
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K) % Send both challenges encrypted
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Mark Ca as secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Mark Cb as secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb') % Authentication property for Cb
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca') % Authentication property for Ca

   % Transition 3: Receive confirmation and complete protocol
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>

end role

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB) % Responder role instance

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

def=

  const a, b          : agent,       % Honest agents

        ca, cb        : protocol_id   % Protocol identifiers

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi} % Intruder's initial knowledge

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)    % Honest session between A and B
    /\  session(a,i,kai) % Session between A and intruder
    /\  session(i,b,kbi) % Session between intruder and B

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Secrecy of initiator's challenges

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca            % Authentication of initiator by responder

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE

%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}

%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
