%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---
inputdata.code:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  %% Local variables
  local  State: nat,         %% State machine variable
         Na,Ca: text,        %% Nonce and Challenge created by A
         Cb   : text,        %% Challenge received from B
         X,K  : message      %% X is B's DH public value, K is session key

  %% Protocol identifiers for secrecy goals
  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  %% Initial state
  init   State := 0

  transition

   %% Transition 1: A generates a nonce Na and sends its exponentiated
   %% value with the shared key Kab to B, initiating the key exchange.
   %% A -> B: exp(Kab, Na)
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   %% Transition 2: A receives B's public value X' (= exp(Kab, Nb)),
   %% computes the session key K, generates a new challenge Ca,
   %% and sends the challenge to B encrypted with the new key K.
   %% A -> B: {Ca'}_K'
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   %% Transition 3: A receives its own challenge Ca back, paired with B's
   %% challenge Cb', encrypted with K. A then sends back Cb' encrypted with K
   %% to authenticate itself to B and confirm the key establishment.
   %% A -> B: {Cb'}_K'
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           
  %% Local variables
  local State: nat,          %% State machine variable
        Nb,Cb: text,         %% Nonce and Challenge created by B
        Ca   : text,         %% Challenge received from A
        Y,K  : message       %% Y is A's DH public value, K is session key

  %% Protocol identifiers for secrecy goals
  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  %% Initial state
  init  State := 0

  transition

   %% Transition 1: B receives A's public value Y' (= exp(Kab, Na)),
   %% generates its own nonce Nb, sends its public value to A,
   %% and computes the session key K.
   %% B -> A: exp(Kab, Nb')
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   %% Transition 2: B receives A's encrypted challenge Ca', generates
   %% its own challenge Cb', and sends both back to A, encrypted with K,
   %% to challenge A and prove its own identity.
   %% B -> A: {Cb', Ca'}_K'
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   %% Transition 3: B receives its challenge Cb back from A, encrypted
   %% with K. This serves as A's response and confirms to B that A has
   %% computed the same session key.
   %% A -> B: {Cb}_K
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
```

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   -- K is derived from X' and Na
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})  -- Ca is kept as secret between A and B
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})  -- Cb is kept as secret between A and B
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) -- B sends Nb' encrypted with the shared key
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  -- K is derived from Y' and Nb'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})  -- Ca is kept as secret between A and B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})  -- Cb is kept as secret between A and B
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3  -- No action is needed after receiving Cb

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)   -- Initiating session between A and B
    /\  session(a,i,kai) -- Initiating session with intruder i
    /\  session(i,b,kbi) -- Initiating session with intruder i and B

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
```

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---

%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% This role represents the initiator of the SPEKE protocol
% A - The initiator agent
% B - The responder agent
% Kab - The shared password between A and B
% Snd,Rcv - Communication channels
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,      % Na - initiator's random exponent, Ca - initiator's challenge
         Cb   : text,      % Cb - responder's challenge
         X,K  : message    % X - received message, K - shared key

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secret goals

  init   State := 0

  transition

   % Transition 1: Initiator sends its Diffie-Hellman value exp(Kab, Na)
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   % Transition 2: Initiator receives responder's DH value, computes shared key K,
   % and sends its encrypted challenge Ca
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)  % Computing the shared key K = exp(exp(Kab,Nb),Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})  % Secrecy goal for Ca
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')  % For authentication: A asserts to have sent Ca to B

   % Transition 3: Initiator receives responder's challenge Cb and confirms
   % its own challenge Ca, then responds with encrypted Cb
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})  % Secrecy goal for Cb
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')  % Authentication goal: A checks if B has sent Cb

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
% This role represents the responder of the SPEKE protocol
% A - The initiator agent
% B - The responder agent
% Kab - The shared password between A and B
% Snd,Rcv - Communication channels
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,      % Nb - responder's random exponent, Cb - responder's challenge
        Ca   : text,      % Ca - initiator's challenge
        Y,K  : message    % Y - received message, K - shared key

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secret goals

  init  State := 0

  transition

   % Transition 1: Responder receives initiator's DH value, generates its own random Nb,
   % sends its DH value and computes the shared key K
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Computing the shared key K = exp(exp(Kab,Na),Nb)

   % Transition 2: Responder receives initiator's encrypted challenge Ca,
   % generates its own challenge Cb, and sends both encrypted with K
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})  % Secrecy goal for Ca
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})  % Secrecy goal for Cb
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')  % For authentication: B asserts to have sent Cb to A
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')  % Authentication goal: B checks if A has sent Ca

   % Transition 3: Responder receives confirmation of its challenge Cb
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% This role represents a session between agents A and B using the shared key Kab
role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Communication channels for the agents

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Instantiate initiator role
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Instantiate responder role

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

% This role defines the global environment including intruder knowledge
role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,           % Honest agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,   % Shared keys (kab between honest agents, 
                                         % kai/kbi between honest agent and intruder)
        ca, cb        : protocol_id      % Protocol IDs for authentication goals

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Initial knowledge of the intruder (i)

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)                  % Honest session between a and b
    /\  session(a,i,kai)                 % Session between honest a and intruder i
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)                 % Session between intruder i and honest b

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,   % Initiator's secrecy goals
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb    % Responder's secrecy goals

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb            % Authentication goal: initiator authenticates responder's challenge

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca            % Authentication goal: responder authenticates initiator's challenge

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
```

inputdata.codenote:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,          % State variable to track the protocol state
         Na,Ca: text,        % Nonce for A (Na) and challenge for A (Ca)
         Cb   : text,        % Challenge for B (Cb)
         X,K  : message       % Received message (X) and computed key (K)

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init   State := 0           % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|>  % Transition from state 0 upon receiving 'start'
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()     % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for A
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na'))  % Send the encrypted nonce to B
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving message from B
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()     % Generate challenge for A
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)   % Compute the key K using received X'
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na)) % Send the challenge encrypted with the key
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')   % Witness that A knows Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from B
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)          % Send the encrypted challenge Cb' back to B
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')   % Request to authenticate challenge Cb'

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat,       % State variable for B's protocol state
        Nb,Cb: text,     % Nonce for B (Nb) and challenge for B (Cb)
        Ca   : text,     % Challenge received from A (Ca)
        Y,K  : message    % Received message (Y) and computed key (K)

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id  % Protocol IDs for secrets

  init  State := 0        % Initializing the state to 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|>   % Transition from state 0 upon receiving reply from A
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()   % Move to state 1 and generate new nonce for B
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb')) % Send the encrypted nonce to A
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')  % Compute the key K using received Y'

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 2 upon receiving challenge from A
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()       % Generate challenge for B
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)   % Send back Cb' encrypted with the key K
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B}) % Declare Ca' as a secret
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B}) % Declare Cb' as a secret
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')   % Witness that B knows Cb'
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')   % Request to authenticate challenge Ca'

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|>  % Transition to state 3 upon receiving response from A
      State':= 3  % Protocol is complete for B

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)  % Define channels for communication

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)  % Initializing Alice's protocol
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)  % Initializing Bob's protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,            % Define agents
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,    % Define symmetric keys
        ca, cb        : protocol_id       % Define protocol IDs for challenges

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}  % Knowledge of the intruder

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)          % Session between Alice (a) and Bob (b)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)      % Session between Alice (a) and the intruder (i)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)      % Session between the intruder (i) and Bob (b)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,  % Ensure the secrets remain confidential
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb  % Ensure initiation authenticates response

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca  % Ensure response authenticates initiation

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
inputdata.logic:
---

**Protocol Description: SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Authentication)**

**Purpose**:  
The SPEKE (Strong Password-Only Key Exchange) protocol facilitates a secure key exchange between two parties, Alice and Bob, using only a shared password for authentication. The protocol ensures that both parties can establish a common secret key without transmitting the password directly, thus protecting it from potential eavesdroppers.

**Roles**:  
1. **Alice (A)**: Initiates the communication and starts the key exchange process.
2. **Bob (B)**: Responds to Alice's requests and participates in the key exchange.

**Key Operations**:  
The communication between Alice and Bob consists of several key operations:

1. **Key Exchange Initialization**:
   - Alice sends a message to Bob containing an exponentiation of the shared password (S(A,B)) and a nonce (Na) which ensures freshness and prevents replay attacks.
   - Bob responds with a similar message, including his nonce (Nb).

2. **Key Computation**:
   - Both Alice and Bob compute the shared session key (K) using their received nonces and the shared password:  
     \( K = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Na), Nb) = \text{exp}(\text{exp}(S(A,B), Nb), Na) \).

3. **Challenge/Response Authentication**:
   - Alice sends a challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key (K).
   - Bob responds with his challenge (Cb) and Alice's challenge (Ca) encrypted with the session key.
   - Finally, Alice sends Bob's challenge (Cb) encrypted with the session key to complete the authentication process.

**Secret Management**:  
- Both parties maintain secrets (Ca and Cb) that are tied to their identities and are used to establish the authenticity of messages exchanged during the protocol.
- Each party is aware of their secrets and utilizes them for mutual authentication, ensuring that they can verify the identity of each other.

**Security Goals**:  
1. **Confidentiality**:  
   - The secrets (Ca and Cb) are kept confidential and are not disclosed to unauthorized parties.
   
2. **Authentication**:  
   - Both Alice and Bob authenticate each other through the exchange of encrypted challenges, ensuring that they are communicating with the intended party and not an imposter.

**Limitations**:  
- There are no known limitations to this protocol as per the current implementation.

**Classification**:  
- The protocol falls under classifications G2 and G12, which pertain to message authentication and confidentiality concerns.

**Known Attacks**:  
- The protocol has been evaluated and is reported to be resistant to known attacks at this time.

**References**:  
- For a detailed understanding and mathematical foundations of the SPEKE protocol, refer to the original work by Jablon: [Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange](http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html).

---
inputdata.code:
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
        
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')

   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')

   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca

end goal  
 
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()

Below is your HLPSL file with added comments, enclosed between ```hlpsl and ``` as requested. The original code structure and logic are preserved, and the comments provide clear explanations of each role, transition, and section.

```hlpsl
%% PROTOCOL*: SPEKE
%% VARIANT: SPEKE (with strong password-only authentication)
%% PURPOSE: Strong Password-Only Authenticated Key Exchange
%% REFERENCE:
%% \url{http://citeseer.ist.psu.edu/jablon96strong.html}
%% MODELER:
%% \begin{itemize}
%%  \item Haykal Tej, Siemens CT IC 3, 2003
%%  \item Sebastian M{\"o}dersheim, ETH Z{\"u}rich, December 2003
%% \end{itemize}
%%
%% ALICE_BOB:
%% \begin{verbatim}
%% A -> B : exp(S(A,B), Na)     |    key exchange part
%% B -> A : exp(S(A,B), Nb)     |
%%
%%               both A and B compute
%%               K = exp(exp(S(A,B),Na), Nb) = exp(exp(S(A,B),Nb), Na)
%%
%% A -> B : {Ca}_K              |
%% B -> A : {Cb,Ca}_K           |    challenge/response
%% A -> B : {Cb}_K              |    authentication part
%%
%% S(A,B): password (shared key)
%% \end{verbatim}
%%
%% LIMITATIONS: None
%%
%% PROBLEMS: 3
%%
%% CLASSIFICATION: G2 G12
%%
%% ATTACKS: None
%% NOTES: None
%%	
%%HLPSL:
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Init (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by A
def=
                 
  local  State: nat,
         Na,Ca: text,
         Cb   : text,
         X,K  : message

  const  sec_i_Ca, sec_i_Cb : protocol_id

  init   State := 0

  transition

   % Transition 1: Initiator starts the protocol upon receiving a start signal
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(start) =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Na' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Na')) 
      % Generate a fresh nonce Na and send exp(Kab, Na) to B to initiate key exchange

   % Transition 2: Receive B's response, compute the shared key, and send a challenge
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv(X') =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Ca' := new()
                 /\ K' := exp(X',Na)
		 /\ Snd({Ca'}_exp(X',Na))
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_i_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(A,B,ca,Ca')
      % Receive X from B, compute K = exp(X, Na), generate challenge Ca,
      % send {Ca}_K to B, mark Ca as secret between A and B,
      % and provide witness for authentication of Ca

   % Transition 3: Verify B's response and complete authentication
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb'.Ca}_K ) =|> 
      State':= 3 /\ Snd({Cb'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_i_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ request(A,B,cb,Cb')
      % Receive {Cb, Ca}_K from B, send {Cb}_K back to confirm,
      % mark Cb as secret between A and B, and request authentication on Cb

end role

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%
role speke_Resp (A,B: agent,
                 Kab: symmetric_key,
                 Snd,Rcv: channel(dy))
played_by B
def=
           

  local State: nat, 
        Nb,Cb: text,
        Ca   : text,
        Y,K  : message

  const sec_r_Ca, sec_r_Cb : protocol_id

  init  State := 0

  transition

   % Transition 1: Responder receives A's message and replies with its own
   1. State  = 0 /\ Rcv(Y') =|> 
      State':= 1 /\ Nb' := new()
                 /\ Snd(exp(Kab, Nb'))
		 /\ K' := exp(Y', Nb')
      % Receive Y from A, generate a fresh nonce Nb, send exp(Kab, Nb) to A,
      % and compute K = exp(Y, Nb) for the shared key

   % Transition 2: Receive A's challenge and respond with both challenges
   2. State  = 1 /\ Rcv({Ca'}_K) =|>  
      State':= 2 /\ Cb' := new()
                 /\ Snd({Cb'.Ca'}_K)
		 /\ secret(Ca',sec_r_Ca,{A,B})
		 /\ secret(Cb',sec_r_Cb,{A,B})
		 /\ witness(B,A,cb,Cb')
		 /\ request(B,A,ca,Ca')
      % Receive {Ca}_K from A, generate challenge Cb, send {Cb, Ca}_K to A,
      % mark Ca and Cb as secrets between A and B, witness authentication for Cb,
      % and request authentication for Ca

   % Transition 3: Receive confirmation from A and end the protocol
   3. State  = 2 /\ Rcv({Cb}_K) =|> 
      State':= 3 
      % Receive {Cb}_K from A, confirming authentication, and complete the protocol

end role
%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role session (A,B: agent,
	      Kab: symmetric_key)
def=

   local SA,RA,SB,RB: channel (dy)

   composition

       speke_Init(A,B,Kab,SA,RA)
    /\ speke_Resp(A,B,Kab,SB,RB)

end role
% Define a session between agents A and B with shared key Kab
% Declare local channels for sending (SA, SB) and receiving (RA, RB)
% Compose the initiator (A) and responder (B) roles to execute the protocol

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

role environment()
def=

  const a, b          : agent,
        kab, kai, kbi : symmetric_key,
        ca, cb        : protocol_id

  intruder_knowledge = {a, b, kai, kbi}

  composition
	session(a,b,kab)
    /\  session(a,i,kai)
    /\  session(i,b,kbi)

end role
% Define the top-level environment for protocol execution
% Declare agents a, b, and intruder i, with shared keys kab (a-b), kai (a-i), kbi (i-b)
% Specify intruder's initial knowledge: agents a, b and keys kai, kbi
% Compose three sessions: a-b (normal execution), a-i, and i-b (intruder interactions)

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

goal

   % Confidentiality (G12)
   %secrecy_of Ca, Cb 
   secrecy_of sec_i_Ca,sec_i_Cb,
              sec_r_Ca,sec_r_Cb
   % Ensure secrecy of challenges Ca and Cb, represented by constants
   % sec_i_Ca (Initiator's Ca), sec_i_Cb (Initiator's Cb),
   % sec_r_Ca (Responder's Ca), sec_r_Cb (Responder's Cb)

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Init authenticates SPEKE_Resp on cb
   authentication_on cb
   % Verify that Initiator (A) authenticates Responder (B) on challenge Cb

   % Message Authentication (G2)
   % SPEKE_Resp authenticates SPEKE_Init on ca
   authentication_on ca
   % Verify that Responder (B) authenticates Initiator (A) on challenge Ca

end goal  

%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%%

environment()
```
