fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .
  var NA NB N : Nonce .
  var GE : GenvExp .
  var G : Gen .
  vars A B : Name .
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .
  var Ke : Key .
  vars XE YE : Exp .
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .
  var Sr : Secret .

  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] &
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]
  [nonexec] .

  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol

  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information

  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- Names are public
  subsort Gen < Public . --- Generator is public

  --- Secret: Definition of a secret between two names
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator: Generates a nonce related to a Name
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder: Represents different roles within the protocol
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption: Define encryption operation 
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exponentiation: Define exponentiation operation
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Generator: Defines the generator in the protocol
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet: A set of nonces
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .

   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .

  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .       --- Nonces storage
  var NA NB N : Nonce .                    --- Variables for Nonces
  var GE : GenvExp .                       --- Variable for GenvExp
  var G : Gen .                            --- Variable for generator
  vars A B : Name .                        --- Variables for participants
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .             --- Freshness variables for nonces
  var Ke : Key .                           --- Key used in encryption
  vars XE YE : Exp .                       --- Exponentiation variables
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .                     --- Message storage variables
  var Sr : Secret .                        --- Secret storage variable

  --- Definition of Dolev-Yao strands
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &   --- Message M1 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &   --- Message M2 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] & --- Messages combined
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Encryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Decryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] & --- Nonce combination
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] & --- Exponentiation
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &          --- Creating a nonce
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &               --- Exposing the generator
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]                --- Exposing participant A
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of the protocol strands
  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of extra grammars for protocol
  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of attack states
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

inputdata.logic:
### Protocol Description

#### Overview
The protocol facilitates secure communication between two parties, Alice (A) and Bob (B), using nonces and a shared secret. The protocol ensures confidentiality and authentication through the use of encryption and exponentiation.

#### Participants
- **Alice (A)**: One of the communicating parties.
- **Bob (B)**: The other communicating party.
- **Intruder (I)**: An adversary attempting to intercept and manipulate the messages between Alice and Bob.

#### Messages
1. **Message 1**: 
   - **From A to B**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message includes Alice's name, Bob's name, and an exponentiated nonce `N_A`, using a generator `g`.

2. **Message 2**: 
   - **From B to A**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message is a response from Bob to Alice, containing the same exponentiated nonce.

3. **Message 3**: 
   - **From A to B**: `enc(exp(exp(g, N_B), N_A), secret(A, B))`
   - This message contains the encrypted value of a double exponentiation of Bob's nonce `N_B` raised to the generator `g` and nonce `N_A`, encrypted with the shared secret between Alice and Bob.

#### Nonces and Keys
- **Nonces**: `N_A` and `N_B` are unique values generated by Alice and Bob, respectively, to ensure freshness in the communication.
- **Secret**: `secret(A, B)` denotes the shared secret key between Alice and Bob, used in the encryption process.

#### Algebraic Properties
The protocol utilizes several algebraic properties for encryption and exponentiation:
- **Exponentiation**: 
   - `exp(exp(X, Y), Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)` where `*` denotes the XOR operation.
- **Encryption and Decryption**:
   - `e(K, d(K, M)) = M`: Decrypting a message `M` encrypted with key `K` returns the original message.
   - `d(K, e(K, M)) = M`: Encrypting a message `M` with key `K` and then decrypting it returns the original message.

### Protocol Properties
1. **Confidentiality**: Only Alice and Bob can read the messages exchanged due to encryption.
2. **Authentication**: The use of nonces ensures that each session is unique and prevents replay attacks.
3. **Integrity**: The shared secret ensures that messages are not tampered with during transmission.

### Conclusion
This protocol is designed to enable secure communication between two parties while ensuring that the messages are confidential and authenticated. The use of nonces, encryption, and algebraic properties of exponentiation forms the core of its security model.

### Corresponding Maude File
The provided Maude file contains the necessary definitions and algebraic properties that implement the above protocol in a formal manner, ensuring it can be analyzed for security properties using the Maude-NPA tool.

      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of attack states
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

inputdata.logic:
### Protocol Description

#### Overview
The protocol facilitates secure communication between two parties, Alice (A) and Bob (B), using nonces and a shared secret. The protocol ensures confidentiality and authentication through the use of encryption and exponentiation.

#### Participants
- **Alice (A)**: One of the communicating parties.
- **Bob (B)**: The other communicating party.
- **Intruder (I)**: An adversary attempting to intercept and manipulate the messages between Alice and Bob.

#### Messages
1. **Message 1**: 
   - **From A to B**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message includes Alice's name, Bob's name, and an exponentiated nonce `N_A`, using a generator `g`.

2. **Message 2**: 
   - **From B to A**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message is a response from Bob to Alice, containing the same exponentiated nonce.

3. **Message 3**: 
   - **From A to B**: `enc(exp(exp(g, N_B), N_A), secret(A, B))`
   - This message contains the encrypted value of a double exponentiation of Bob's nonce `N_B` raised to the generator `g` and nonce `N_A`, encrypted with the shared secret between Alice and Bob.

#### Nonces and Keys
- **Nonces**: `N_A` and `N_B` are unique values generated by Alice and Bob, respectively, to ensure freshness in the communication.
- **Secret**: `secret(A, B)` denotes the shared secret key between Alice and Bob, used in the encryption process.

#### Algebraic Properties
The protocol utilizes several algebraic properties for encryption and exponentiation:
- **Exponentiation**: 
   - `exp(exp(X, Y), Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)` where `*` denotes the XOR operation.
- **Encryption and Decryption**:
   - `e(K, d(K, M)) = M`: Decrypting a message `M` encrypted with key `K` returns the original message.
   - `d(K, e(K, M)) = M`: Encrypting a message `M` with key `K` and then decrypting it returns the original message.

### Protocol Properties
1. **Confidentiality**: Only Alice and Bob can read the messages exchanged due to encryption.
2. **Authentication**: The use of nonces ensures that each session is unique and prevents replay attacks.
3. **Integrity**: The shared secret ensures that messages are not tampered with during transmission.

### Conclusion
This protocol is designed to enable secure communication between two parties while ensuring that the messages are confidential and authenticated. The use of nonces, encryption, and algebraic properties of exponentiation forms the core of its security model.

### Corresponding Maude File
The provided Maude file contains the necessary definitions and algebraic properties that implement the above protocol in a formal manner, ensuring it can be analyzed for security properties using the Maude-NPA tool.
inputdata.code:
***(
The informal journal-level description of this protocol is as follows:

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
B --> A: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
A --> B: enc(exp(exp(g,N_B),N_A),secret(A,B))

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encripted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encription/decription have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appopriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary
  subsort Gen < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary

  --- Secret
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exp
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Gen
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Concatenation
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

endfm

```maude
***(
The informal journal-level description of this protocol is as follows:

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
B --> A: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
A --> B: enc(exp(exp(g,N_B),N_A),secret(A,B))

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encripted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encription/decription have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appopriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary
  subsort Gen < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary

  --- Secret
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exp
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Gen
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Concatenation
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-ALGEBRAIC is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the algebraic properties 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .
  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .
  var NA NB N : Nonce .
  var GE : GenvExp .
  var G : Gen .
  vars A B : Name .
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .
  var Ke : Key .
  vars XE YE : Exp .
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .
  var Sr : Secret .

  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] &
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]
  [nonexec] .

  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is

  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator: Generates a nonce related to a Name
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder: Represents different roles within the protocol
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption: Define encryption operation

  --- Exponentiation: Define exponentiation operation
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .       --- Nonces storage
  var NA NB N : Nonce .                    --- Variables for Nonces
  var GE : GenvExp .                       --- Variable for GenvExp
  var G : Gen .                            --- Variable for generator
  vars A B : Name .                        --- Variables for participants
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .             --- Freshness variables for nonces
  var Ke : Key .                           --- Key used in encryption
  vars XE YE : Exp .                       --- Exponentiation variables
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .                     --- Message storage variables
  var Sr : Secret .                        --- Secret storage variable

  --- Definition of Dolev-Yao strands
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &   --- Message M1 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &   --- Message M2 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] & --- Messages combined
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Encryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Decryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] & --- Nonce combination
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] & --- Exponentiation
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &          --- Creating a nonce
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &               --- Exposing the generator
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]                --- Exposing participant A
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of the protocol strands
  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of extra grammars for protocol
  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of attack states
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

inputdata.logic:
### Protocol Description

#### Overview
The protocol facilitates secure communication between two parties, Alice (A) and Bob (B), using nonces and a shared secret. The protocol ensures confidentiality and authentication through the use of encryption and exponentiation.

#### Participants
- **Alice (A)**: One of the communicating parties.
- **Bob (B)**: The other communicating party.
- **Intruder (I)**: An adversary attempting to intercept and manipulate the messages between Alice and Bob.

#### Messages
1. **Message 1**: 
   - **From A to B**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message includes Alice's name, Bob's name, and an exponentiated nonce `N_A`, using a generator `g`.

2. **Message 2**: 
   - **From B to A**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message is a response from Bob to Alice, containing the same exponentiated nonce.

3. **Message 3**: 
   - **From A to B**: `enc(exp(exp(g, N_B), N_A), secret(A, B))`
   - This message contains the encrypted value of a double exponentiation of Bob's nonce `N_B` raised to the generator `g` and nonce `N_A`, encrypted with the shared secret between Alice and Bob.

#### Nonces and Keys
- **Nonces**: `N_A` and `N_B` are unique values generated by Alice and Bob, respectively, to ensure freshness in the communication.
- **Secret**: `secret(A, B)` denotes the shared secret key between Alice and Bob, used in the encryption process.

#### Algebraic Properties
The protocol utilizes several algebraic properties for encryption and exponentiation:
- **Exponentiation**: 
   - `exp(exp(X, Y), Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)` where `*` denotes the XOR operation.
- **Encryption and Decryption**:
   - `e(K, d(K, M)) = M`: Decrypting a message `M` encrypted with key `K` returns the original message.
   - `d(K, e(K, M)) = M`: Encrypting a message `M` with key `K` and then decrypting it returns the original message.

### Protocol Properties
1. **Confidentiality**: Only Alice and Bob can read the messages exchanged due to encryption.
2. **Authentication**: The use of nonces ensures that each session is unique and prevents replay attacks.
3. **Integrity**: The shared secret ensures that messages are not tampered with during transmission.

### Conclusion
This protocol is designed to enable secure communication between two parties while ensuring that the messages are confidential and authenticated. The use of nonces, encryption, and algebraic properties of exponentiation forms the core of its security model.

### Corresponding Maude File
The provided Maude file contains the necessary definitions and algebraic properties that implement the above protocol in a formal manner, ensuring it can be analyzed for security properties using the Maude-NPA tool.

message y encrypted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encryption/decryption have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appropriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.

  subsort Gen < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary

inputdata.codenote:
***(
The informal journal-level description of this protocol is as follows:

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
B --> A: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
A --> B: enc(exp(exp(g,N_B),N_A),secret(A,B))

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encrypted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encryption/decryption have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appropriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- Names are public
  subsort Gen < Public . --- Generator is public

  --- Secret: Definition of a secret between two names
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator: Generates a nonce related to a Name
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder: Represents different roles within the protocol
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption: Define encryption operation 
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exponentiation: Define exponentiation operation
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Generator: Defines the generator in the protocol
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet: A set of nonces
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Concatenation: Defines how messages are concatenated 
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .

  --- Overwrite this module with the algebraic properties

  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .

  var G : Gen .                            --- Variable for generator
  vars A B : Name .                        --- Variables for participants
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .             --- Freshness variables for nonces
  var Ke : Key .                           --- Key used in encryption
  vars XE YE : Exp .                       --- Exponentiation variables
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .                     --- Message storage variables
  var Sr : Secret .                        --- Secret storage variable

  --- Definition of Dolev-Yao strands
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &   --- Message M1 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &   --- Message M2 created

      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r ::

      (:: nil ::

     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

inputdata.logic:
### Protocol Description

#### Overview
The protocol facilitates secure communication between two parties, Alice (A) and Bob (B), using nonces and a shared secret. The protocol ensures confidentiality and authentication through the use of encryption and exponentiation.

#### Participants
- **Alice (A)**: One of the communicating parties.
- **Bob (B)**: The other communicating party.
- **Intruder (I)**: An adversary attempting to intercept and manipulate the messages between Alice and Bob.

#### Messages
1. **Message 1**: 
   - **From A to B**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message includes Alice's name, Bob's name, and an exponentiated nonce `N_A`, using a generator `g`.

2. **Message 2**: 
   - **From B to A**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message is a response from Bob to Alice, containing the same exponentiated nonce.

3. **Message 3**: 
   - **From A to B**: `enc(exp(exp(g, N_B), N_A), secret(A, B))`
   - This message contains the encrypted value of a double exponentiation of Bob's nonce `N_B` raised to the generator `g` and nonce `N_A`, encrypted with the shared secret between Alice and Bob.

#### Nonces and Keys
- **Nonces**: `N_A` and `N_B` are unique values generated by Alice and Bob, respectively, to ensure freshness in the communication.
- **Secret**: `secret(A, B)` denotes the shared secret key between Alice and Bob, used in the encryption process.

#### Algebraic Properties
The protocol utilizes several algebraic properties for encryption and exponentiation:
- **Exponentiation**: 
   - `exp(exp(X, Y), Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)` where `*` denotes the XOR operation.
- **Encryption and Decryption**:
   - `e(K, d(K, M)) = M`: Decrypting a message `M` encrypted with key `K` returns the original message.
   - `d(K, e(K, M)) = M`: Encrypting a message `M` with key `K` and then decrypting it returns the original message.

### Protocol Properties
1. **Confidentiality**: Only Alice and Bob can read the messages exchanged due to encryption.
2. **Authentication**: The use of nonces ensures that each session is unique and prevents replay attacks.
3. **Integrity**: The shared secret ensures that messages are not tampered with during transmission.

### Conclusion
This protocol is designed to enable secure communication between two parties while ensuring that the messages are confidential and authenticated. The use of nonces, encryption, and algebraic properties of exponentiation forms the core of its security model.

### Corresponding Maude File
The provided Maude file contains the necessary definitions and algebraic properties that implement the above protocol in a formal manner, ensuring it can be analyzed for security properties using the Maude-NPA tool.
inputdata.code:
***(
The informal journal-level description of this protocol is as follows:

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
B --> A: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
A --> B: enc(exp(exp(g,N_B),N_A),secret(A,B))

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encripted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encription/decription have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appopriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary
  subsort Gen < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary

  --- Secret
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exp
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Gen
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Concatenation
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-ALGEBRAIC is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the algebraic properties 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .
  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .
  var NA NB N : Nonce .
  var GE : GenvExp .
  var G : Gen .
  vars A B : Name .
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .
  var Ke : Key .
  vars XE YE : Exp .
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .
  var Sr : Secret .

  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] &
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]
  [nonexec] .

  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)

message y encripted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between

algebraic properties:

  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appopriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

--- This module defines the symbols and sorts used in the protocol specification
fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary
  subsort Gen < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary

  --- Secret operator: represents a shared secret between two principals
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator: represents a fresh nonce generated by a principal
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder and honest principals
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption and decryption operators
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exponentiation operator (for Diffie-Hellman style key exchange)
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Generator for exponentiation
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- Non-empty set of nonces (used for exponentiation)
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Message concatenation
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

--- This module defines the algebraic properties of the protocol
fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-ALGEBRAIC is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the algebraic properties 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  --- Algebraic property for exponentiation: exp(exp(g,y),z) = exp(g, y*z)
  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .
  
  --- Algebraic properties for encryption/decryption
  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

endfm

--- This module specifies the protocol strands and attack states
fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .
  var NA NB N : Nonce .
  var GE : GenvExp .
  var G : Gen .
  vars A B : Name .
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .
  var Ke : Key .
  vars XE YE : Exp .
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .
  var Sr : Secret .

  --- Dolev-Yao intruder capabilities
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &              --- Extract first component
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &              --- Extract second component
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] &       --- Concatenate messages
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] &        --- Encrypt messages
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] &        --- Decrypt messages
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] &   --- Combine nonce sets
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] &    --- Perform exponentiation
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &                      --- Generate intruder nonce
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &                           --- Know generator g
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]                             --- Know principal names
  [nonexec] .

  --- Protocol specification with two roles
  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' ::                                                --- Initiator role (A)
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))),                          --- Send A, B, and g^n_A
            -(A ; B ; XE),                                     --- Receive A, B, and B's exponent
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &             --- Send encrypted secret using shared key
     :: r ::                                                   --- Responder role (B)
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE),                                     --- Receive A, B, and A's exponent
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))),                          --- Send A, B, and g^n_B
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]                      --- Receive encrypted secret
  [nonexec] .

  --- Grammar to constrain the search space and improve efficiency
  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 0: Authentication attack
  --- This checks if B can complete the protocol with a without a having
  --- actually initiated the session (authentication failure)
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 1: Secrecy attack
  --- This checks if the intruder can learn the secret shared between a and b
  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 2: Secrecy attack with state space reduction
  --- Similar to attack state 1 but with additional never patterns to reduce the search space
  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path (repeated exponentiations)
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states (type inconsistency)
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states (decryption with wrong key)
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states (different generators)
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)

     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

inputdata.codenote:
***(
The informal journal-level description of this protocol is as follows:

A --> B: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
B --> A: A ; B ; exp(g,N_A)
A --> B: enc(exp(exp(g,N_B),N_A),secret(A,B))

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encrypted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encryption/decryption have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appropriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.
)***

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the syntax of your protocol
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg and Fresh are special and imported
  --- * Every sort must be a subsort of Msg
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Sort Information 
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  subsort Exp < Key .
  subsort Name < Public . --- Names are public
  subsort Gen < Public . --- Generator is public

  --- Secret: Definition of a secret between two names
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .

  --- Nonce operator: Generates a nonce related to a Name
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder: Represents different roles within the protocol
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption: Define encryption operation 
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exponentiation: Define exponentiation operation
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  --- Generator: Defines the generator in the protocol
  op g : -> Gen .

  --- NeNonceSet: A set of nonces
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .

  --- Concatenation: Defines how messages are concatenated 
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-ALGEBRAIC is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the algebraic properties 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  --- Algebraic property of exponentiation
  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .

  --- Algebraic property of encryption and decryption
  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

endfm

fmod PROTOCOL-SPECIFICATION is
  protecting PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS .
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  protecting DEFINITION-CONSTRAINTS-INPUT .

  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- Overwrite this module with the strands 
  --- of your protocol
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  
  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet .       --- Nonces storage
  var NA NB N : Nonce .                    --- Variables for Nonces
  var GE : GenvExp .                       --- Variable for GenvExp
  var G : Gen .                            --- Variable for generator
  vars A B : Name .                        --- Variables for participants
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .             --- Freshness variables for nonces
  var Ke : Key .                           --- Key used in encryption
  vars XE YE : Exp .                       --- Exponentiation variables
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .                     --- Message storage variables
  var Sr : Secret .                        --- Secret storage variable

  --- Definition of Dolev-Yao strands
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &   --- Message M1 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &   --- Message M2 created
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] & --- Messages combined
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Encryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] & --- Decryption process
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] & --- Nonce combination
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] & --- Exponentiation
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &          --- Creating a nonce
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &               --- Exposing the generator
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]                --- Exposing participant A
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of the protocol strands
  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))), 
            -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE), 
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))), 
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of extra grammars for protocol
  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .  
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Definition of attack states
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || empty
     || nil
     || nil
     || never
     *** Pattern for authentication
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE), 
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE), 
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))), 
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]
     || sec(a,r') inI
     || nil
     || nil
     || never(
     *** Avoid infinite useless path
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Pattern to avoid unreachable states
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

inputdata.logic:
### Protocol Description

#### Overview
The protocol facilitates secure communication between two parties, Alice (A) and Bob (B), using nonces and a shared secret. The protocol ensures confidentiality and authentication through the use of encryption and exponentiation.

#### Participants
- **Alice (A)**: One of the communicating parties.
- **Bob (B)**: The other communicating party.
- **Intruder (I)**: An adversary attempting to intercept and manipulate the messages between Alice and Bob.

#### Messages
1. **Message 1**: 
   - **From A to B**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message includes Alice's name, Bob's name, and an exponentiated nonce `N_A`, using a generator `g`.

2. **Message 2**: 
   - **From B to A**: `A ; B ; exp(g, N_A)`
   - This message is a response from Bob to Alice, containing the same exponentiated nonce.

3. **Message 3**: 
   - **From A to B**: `enc(exp(exp(g, N_B), N_A), secret(A, B))`
   - This message contains the encrypted value of a double exponentiation of Bob's nonce `N_B` raised to the generator `g` and nonce `N_A`, encrypted with the shared secret between Alice and Bob.

#### Nonces and Keys
- **Nonces**: `N_A` and `N_B` are unique values generated by Alice and Bob, respectively, to ensure freshness in the communication.
- **Secret**: `secret(A, B)` denotes the shared secret key between Alice and Bob, used in the encryption process.

#### Algebraic Properties
The protocol utilizes several algebraic properties for encryption and exponentiation:
- **Exponentiation**: 
   - `exp(exp(X, Y), Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)` where `*` denotes the XOR operation.
- **Encryption and Decryption**:
   - `e(K, d(K, M)) = M`: Decrypting a message `M` encrypted with key `K` returns the original message.
   - `d(K, e(K, M)) = M`: Encrypting a message `M` with key `K` and then decrypting it returns the original message.

### Protocol Properties
1. **Confidentiality**: Only Alice and Bob can read the messages exchanged due to encryption.
2. **Authentication**: The use of nonces ensures that each session is unique and prevents replay attacks.
3. **Integrity**: The shared secret ensures that messages are not tampered with during transmission.

### Conclusion
This protocol is designed to enable secure communication between two parties while ensuring that the messages are confidential and authenticated. The use of nonces, encryption, and algebraic properties of exponentiation forms the core of its security model.

### Corresponding Maude File
The provided Maude file contains the necessary definitions and algebraic properties that implement the above protocol in a formal manner, ensuring it can be analyzed for security properties using the Maude-NPA tool.

fmod PROTOCOL-EXAMPLE-SYMBOLS is
  --- Importing sorts Msg, Fresh, Public from DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES
  protecting DEFINITION-PROTOCOL-RULES .
  
  ----------------------------------------------------------
  --- This module defines the basic symbols and sorts for the protocol.
  --- Notes:
  --- * Sort Msg represents messages exchanged in the protocol.
  --- * Sort Fresh represents fresh values (e.g., nonces or session-specific data).
  --- * Sort Public represents publicly known information.
  --- * All custom sorts are subsorts of Msg to ensure they are valid messages.
  --- * No sort can be a supersort of Msg to maintain type hierarchy.
  ----------------------------------------------------------

  --- Defining custom sorts for the protocol
  sorts Name Nonce NeNonceSet Gen Exp Key GenvExp Secret .
  --- Name: Represents participant identities (e.g., A, B, or intruder i).
  --- Nonce: Unique values generated by participants (e.g., N_A, N_B).
  --- NeNonceSet: Sets of nonces combined via XOR (modeled as multiplication).
  --- Gen: Generator values (e.g., g) used in exponentiation.
  --- Exp: Exponentiation results (e.g., g^N_A).
  --- Key: Encryption keys, including exponents used as keys.
  --- GenvExp: General exponentiation type encompassing Gen and Exp.
  --- Secret: Shared secrets between participants (e.g., secret(A,B)).

  --- Subsort relationships:
  --- Gen and Exp are subsorts of GenvExp (general exponentiation type)
  subsort Gen Exp < GenvExp .
  
  --- Name, NeNonceSet, GenvExp, Secret, and Key are subsorts of Msg
  subsort Name NeNonceSet GenvExp Secret Key < Msg .
  
  --- Exp is a subsort of Key, allowing exponents to serve as encryption keys
  subsort Exp < Key .
  
  --- Name and Gen are subsorts of Public, indicating they are publicly known
  subsort Name < Public . --- This is quite relevant and necessary
  subsort Gen < Public .  --- This is quite relevant and necessary

  --- Operator for generating secrets shared between two names
  op sec : Name Fresh -> Secret [frozen] .
  --- 'sec(A, r)' creates a secret tied to name A and fresh value r.

  --- Operator for generating nonces
  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .
  --- 'n(A, r)' generates a nonce for name A using fresh value r.

  --- Constants representing participant names
  ops a b i : -> Name .
  --- 'a' and 'b' are honest participants; 'i' is the intruder.

  --- Encryption and decryption operators
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  --- 'e(K, M)' encrypts message M with key K.
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  --- 'd(K, M)' decrypts message M with key K.

  --- Exponentiation operator
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .
  --- 'exp(GE, NS)' computes GE raised to the power of nonce set NS (e.g., g^N_A).

  --- Constant for the generator
  op g : -> Gen .
  --- 'g' is the base generator for exponentiation.

  --- Nonce sets: Nonce is a subsort of NeNonceSet for singleton sets
  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
  
  --- Operator for combining nonce sets (XOR modeled as multiplication)
  op _*_ : NeNonceSet NeNonceSet -> NeNonceSet [frozen assoc comm] .
  --- '_*_' combines two nonce sets; associative and commutative properties apply.

  --- Operator for message concatenation
  op _;_ : Msg Msg -> Msg [frozen gather (e E)] .
  --- '_;_' concatenates two messages; gather attribute controls parsing.

endfm

  --- Exponentiation property: (W^Y)^Z = W^(Y*Z)
  eq exp(exp(W:Gen,Y:NeNonceSet),Z:NeNonceSet) 
   = exp(W:Gen, Y:NeNonceSet * Z:NeNonceSet) [metadata "variant"] .
  --- Restricted to W:Gen (e.g., g) for finitary unification in Maude-NPA.
  
  --- Encryption/decryption properties:
  --- Decryption undoes encryption
  eq e(K:Key,d(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .
  --- Encryption undoes decryption
  eq d(K:Key,e(K:Key,M:Msg)) = M:Msg [metadata "variant"] .

  vars NS1 NS2 NS3 NS : NeNonceSet . --- Nonce sets
  var NA NB N : Nonce .             --- Individual nonces
  var GE : GenvExp .                --- General exponentiation values
  var G : Gen .                     --- Generators
  vars A B : Name .                 --- Participant names
  vars r r' r1 r2 r3 : Fresh .      --- Fresh values
  var Ke : Key .                    --- Keys
  vars XE YE : Exp .                --- Exponentiation results
  vars M M1 M2 : Msg .              --- Messages
  var Sr : Secret .                 --- Secrets

  --- Dolev-Yao strands: Define intruder capabilities
  eq STRANDS-DOLEVYAO = 
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M1), nil ] &  --- Split M1 from M1 ; M2
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1 ; M2), +(M2), nil ] &  --- Split M2 from M1 ; M2
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(M1), -(M2), +(M1 ; M2), nil ] &  --- Concatenate M1 and M2
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(e(Ke,M)), nil ] &  --- Encrypt M with Ke
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(Ke), -(M), +(d(Ke,M)), nil ] &  --- Decrypt M with Ke
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(NS1), -(NS2), +(NS1 * NS2), nil ] &  --- Combine nonce sets
     :: nil :: [ nil | -(GE), -(NS), +(exp(GE,NS)), nil ] &  --- Compute exponentiation
     :: r ::   [ nil | +(n(i,r)), nil ] &  --- Intruder generates nonce n(i,r)
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(g), nil ] &       --- Intruder knows generator g
     :: nil :: [ nil | +(A), nil ]         --- Intruder knows names
  [nonexec] .

  --- Protocol strands: Define honest participant behavior
  eq STRANDS-PROTOCOL = 
     --- Initiator strand (A)
     :: r,r' :: 
     [nil | +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(A,r))),       --- A sends A ; B ; g^N_A
            -(A ; B ; XE),                  --- A receives A ; B ; g^N_B (as XE)
            +(e(exp(XE,n(A,r)),sec(A,r'))), nil] &  --- A sends e(g^N_B^N_A, sec(A,r'))
     --- Responder strand (B)
     :: r :: 
     [nil | -(A ; B ; XE),                  --- B receives A ; B ; g^N_A (as XE)
            +(A ; B ; exp(g,n(B,r))),       --- B sends A ; B ; g^N_B
            -(e(exp(XE,n(B,r)),Sr)), nil]   --- B receives e(g^N_A^N_B, sec(A,r'))
  [nonexec] .

  --- Extra grammars: Define constraints on nonce sets
  eq EXTRA-GRAMMARS
   = (grl empty => (NS * n(a,r)) inL . ;  --- NS * n(a,r) must be derivable
      grl empty => n(a,r) inL . ;         --- n(a,r) must be derivable
      grl empty => (NS * n(b,r)) inL . ;  --- NS * n(b,r) must be derivable
      grl empty => n(b,r) inL .           --- n(b,r) must be derivable
      ! S2 )
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 0: Authentication goal
  eq ATTACK-STATE(0)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE),                   --- B receives a ; b ; g^N_A
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))),        --- B sends a ; b ; g^N_B
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]  --- B receives e(g^N_A^N_B, sec(a,r'))
     || empty                               --- Intruder has no initial knowledge
     || nil                                 --- No constraints
     || nil                                 --- No never patterns initially
     || never(                              --- Never pattern for authentication
     *** Check if initiator (A) completed its strand
     (:: R:FreshSet ::
     [nil | +(a ; b ; XE),                  --- A sends a ; b ; g^N_A
            -(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))),       --- A receives a ; b ; g^N_B
            +(e(YE,sec(a,r'))), nil]        --- A sends e(g^N_B^N_A, sec(a,r'))
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 1: Secrecy goal for sec(a,r')
  eq ATTACK-STATE(1)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE),                   --- B receives a ; b ; g^N_A
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))),        --- B sends a ; b ; g^N_B
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]  --- B receives e(g^N_A^N_B, sec(a,r'))
     || sec(a,r') inI                       --- Intruder knows sec(a,r')
     || nil                                 --- No constraints
     || nil                                 --- No never patterns
     || nil
  [nonexec] .

  --- Attack state 2: Secrecy with optimization via never patterns
  eq ATTACK-STATE(2)
   = :: r :: 
     [nil, -(a ; b ; XE),                   --- B receives a ; b ; g^N_A
           +(a ; b ; exp(g,n(b,r))),        --- B sends a ; b ; g^N_B
           -(e(exp(XE,n(b,r)),sec(a,r'))) | nil]  --- B receives e(g^N_A^N_B, sec(a,r'))
     || sec(a,r') inI                       --- Intruder knows sec(a,r')
     || nil                                 --- No constraints
     || nil                                 --- No never patterns initially
     || never(                              --- Never patterns to prune search space
     *** Avoid infinite exponentiation paths
      (:: nil :: 
      [ nil | -(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2)), -(NS3), 
          +(exp(GE,NS1 * NS2 * NS3)), nil ] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Avoid unreachable encryption states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
             -(sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh)), 
             +(e(exp(#1:Exp, N2:Nonce), sec(A:Name, #2:Fresh))), nil] 
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Avoid unreachable decryption states
     (:: nil :: 
      [nil | -(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce)), 
          -(e(exp(#1:Exp, N1:Nonce), S:Secret)), 
          +(S:Secret), nil]
      & S:StrandSet || K:IntruderKnowledge)
     *** Avoid states with inconsistent generators
     (S:StrandSet 
      || (#4:Gen != #0:Gen), K:IntruderKnowledge)
     )
  [nonexec] .

endfm

where N_A and N_B are nonces, exp(x,y) means x raised to y, enc(x,y) means
message y encrypted using key x, and secret(A,B) is a secret shared between
A and B. Moreover, exponentiation and encryption/decryption have the following 
algebraic properties:

  exp(exp(X,Y),Z) = exp(X, Y * Z)
  e(K,d(K,M)) = M .
  d(K,e(K,M)) = M .

where * is the xor operator, though no algebraic property is given, since 
they are not necessary for this protocol. However, note that the property 
for exponentiation is restricted below by using appropriate sorts
in such a way that variable X can be only the generator g. This is necessary
to have a finitary unification procedure based on narrowing.

  subsort Name < Public . --- Names are public

  op n : Name Fresh -> Nonce [frozen] .

  --- Intruder: Represents different roles within the protocol
  ops a b i : -> Name .

  --- Encryption: Define encryption operation 
  op e : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .
  op d : Key Msg -> Msg [frozen] .

  --- Exponentiation: Define exponentiation operation
  op exp : GenvExp NeNonceSet -> Exp [frozen] .

  op g : -> Gen .

  subsort Nonce < NeNonceSet .
